Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Records Lodged With The Court In Support Of Deft'S Oppos To Motiuon For Class Certification
Off Calendar Mar-23-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Class Certification, Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Mar-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the following motions:
(1) Plaintiff's motion for class certification;
(2) Defendant's motion to strike class allegations, or in the alternative, to deny class certification;
(3) Defendant's motion to seal records lodged with the Court in support of Apple's motion to strike class allegations;
(4) Defendant's motion to seal records lodged with the Court in connection with defendant Apple's opposition to motion for class certification;
(5) Defendant's motion to seal records lodged with the Court in supoort of plaintiff Steve Camuti's motion for class certification and
(6) Defendant's motion to compel further responses to production of documents, set # one.

Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, #4780.

Matthew R. Bainer and Molly DeSario appeared for plaintiff Steve Camuti, individually and on behalf of all others similarly situated.
Lynne C. Hermle and Julia Collins Riechert appeared for defendant Apple, Inc.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

Defendant's motions to (A) seal records lodged with the Court in support of Apple's motion to strike class allegations, (B) seal records lodged with the Court in connection with defendant Apple's opposition to motion for class certification and (C) seal records lodged with the Court in supoort of plaintiff Steve Camuti's motion for class certification are GRANTED. Orders were submitted to and signed by the Court. All three Orders were personally served on counsel by the Clerk in Department 611.

Plaintiff Steve Camuti's motion for class certification is SUBMITTED. The Court shall prepare the Order After Hearing.

Defendant Apple, Inc.'s motion to strike class allegations is SUBMITTED. The Court shall prepare the Order After Hearing.

Defendant Apple, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to production of documents, set number one, is CONTINUED to April 8, 2011 @ 1:30 p.m.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on plaintiff's motion to compel futher responses to requests for production of documents, and for sanctions.
Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780.

Molly DeSario, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Steve Camuti, individually and on behalf of all others similarly situated.
Julia Reichert, Esq. appeared for defendant Apple, Inc.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is GRANTED in part as to Request Numbers 101 & 103. Current materials shall be delivered to counsel for plaintiffs within five days of today's date, close of business, 4:30 p.m.
Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the Order After Hearing.

Off Calendar Jan-19-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on regularly as scheduled this day, having been continued from January 11, 2011, for hearing on the following: (1) defendant Apple, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to request for admissions and form interrogatories; (2) plaintiff Steve Camuti's motion to compel production of documents and request for sanctions and (3) Case Management Conference.
Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, #4780.

Molly A. DeSario appeared for plaintiff Steve Camuti, individually and on behalf of all others similarly situated.
Julia Collins Riechert and Jessica Perry appeared for defendant Apple, Inc.

After reviewing the papers submitted, hearing argument from counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court GRANTS defendant Apple, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to request for admissions and form interrogatories. Additionally, the Court GRANTS plaintiff Steve Camuti's motion to compel production of documents subjected to reasonable constraints re: time. Counsel to meet and confer on proposed order. Case management issues were addressed at the last calling of the case.

Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Set For Case Management Conference On Jun-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice And Motion For Reconsideration Of Commissioner Hewlett'S Discovery Order
Off Calendar Oct-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED.

Defendant Apple, Inc.'s Motion For Reconsideration is granted. While this court has interpreted Nach & Lewis Architects, Inc. v Superior Court as not being dispositive on the issue of Form Interrogatory 12.2, the Coito decision made it clear that 12.2 was proper. While this court did not base its decision upon Coito, the Supreme Court's grant of review of that decision made it clear that any decision overruling privilege objections to interrogatory 12.2 must be held in abeyance pending disposition of the appeal.

The hearing on this motion has been assigned to Comm. Hewlett in Room 612. Comm. Hewlett has been unexpectedly called away from the court. The court will contact counsel upon his return to schedule a hearing if oral argument is requested. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
